objective: Create Demo Checkpoint 1 PR 1 as a spec/source-facts/governance-only slice. Positive claims: C1-SPEC-001 official source facts refreshed with URLs and verified dates; C1-SPEC-002 future PR contracts defined for latency/capacity/cost/access/quota/cache/pre-generation/retention/launch-level controls, provider abstraction plus real TTS, avatar/video provider, and hosted-demo access/quota/retention/demo-polish; C1-SPEC-003 first-month controlled demo target $30-$60, approval ceiling $75-$200, view-first pre-generated media path, hard quota/retry/cost guardrails recorded; C1-SPEC-004 security/privacy/consent/eval failure matrix IDs are explicit enough for later PRs to map to tests, gates, source facts, human-only rows, or documented non-goals. Negative invariants: NONGOAL-RUNTIME-001 no backend/frontend/provider/RAG/avatar/database/Docker/hosted deployment/provider SDK/provider key/runtime changes; NONGOAL-MEDIA-001 no real audio/video generation or cloned identity implementation; NONGOAL-LAUNCH-001 no Product Mode 2, public distribution, or production-readiness claim. Failure matrix IDs expected in docs: UPLOAD-VALIDATION-001, PROMPT-INJECTION-001, UNSUPPORTED-CLAIMS-001, CITATION-MISMATCH-001, SOURCE-EVAL-MEDIA-MISMATCH-001, MULTILINGUAL-DRIFT-001, PROVIDER-FAILURE-001, QUOTA-EXHAUSTION-001, BACKPRESSURE-RATE-LIMIT-001, CONSENT-REVOKED-001, CLONE-PROFILE-DELETION-001, RETENTION-DELETION-001, DISCLOSURE-VISIBILITY-001, EXTERNAL-INVITE-BOUNDARY-001. Expected evidence: docs gates via guardrails and make quality; tests limited to existing guardrail/phase1 docs tests; official source URLs for provider/platform facts; fan-out adversarial review findings and dispositions.
